text_styles.sample_text: Paste feeds. Paperboy does the setup.

Paperboy works from public RSS and Atom feeds. It validates each URL, reads a bounded set of recent items, removes duplicates, and ranks the result against your filter. No Gmail connection or manual c

text_styles.sample_text: What does automatic setup actually do?

text_styles.font_family: Newsreader, "Sitka Display", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Georgia, serif
text_styles.sample_text: Do I need to connect Gmail?
No. Paste public RSS or Atom feed URLs. Paperboy reads only those URLs.

text_styles.font_family: Newsreader, "Sitka Display", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Georgia, serif
text_styles.sample_text: Does Paperboy auto-subscribe me to newsletters?
No. Many newsletters require email confirmation or expose no public feed. Use a public newsletter archive/feed when available; email-only ingestion is not part of the live preview.

text_styles.font_family: Newsreader, "Sitka Display", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Georgia, serif

text_styles.sample_text: Public RSS and Atom feeds, including Hacker News and many blogs, release feeds, and research feeds.

text_styles.font_family: Newsreader, "Sitka Display", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Georgia, serif

text_styles.sample_text: Yes. Paperboy validates the feeds, scans recent items, removes duplicates, and ranks them against the focus and ignore terms you provide.

text_styles.font_family: Newsreader, "Sitka Display", "Iowan Old Style", "Palatino Linotype", Georgia, serif
text_styles.sample_text: What happens after the free first brief?
If it is useful, you can join the $49/month founding pilot for daily delivery. No card is collected by this page.
